person_name: Longinus (Cassius Longinus), philosopher and rhetorician (c. 213–273 CE)
person_name_full: Longinus (Cassius Longinus), philosopher and rhetorician (c. 213–273 CE)
notes: Philosopher and rhetorician known for critical skill and learning, associated with Neoplatonism in the 3rd century CE.

docs.title: Life of Aurelian
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: … people.⁠ 105 3 It was regarded as a cruel thing that Longinus the philosopher⁠ 106 should have been among those who were killed. He, it is said, was employed by Zenobia as her teacher in Greek letters, and Aurelian is said to have slain him because he was told that that over-proud letter of

docs.title: Letter CXXV
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: "To judge like Longinus" became a synonym for accurate discrimination. A martinet of the old school, who did his utmost to oppose what he considered the luxury of his age. He was censor in 184 b.c. Lucr. V. 905, Munro.
